channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Imane Khelif receives 4 votes for AP's Female Athlete of the Year
item.description: Imane Khelif, the Algerian boxer who is said to have failed previous gender tests in boxing competition, received four votes as AP's Female Athlete of the Year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eight new WNBA coaches now in place. In 2025, 7 women and 6 men will be in charge on the bench
item.description: The WNBA now has all of its head coaching spots filled after there were eight open positions this offseason. Dallas named Chris Koclanes as its head coach on Monday and then an hour later Washington hired Sydney Johnson as its new head, rounding out the seven hirings.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Caitlin Clark honored as AP Female Athlete of the Year following her impact on women's sports
item.description: Caitlin Clark raised the profile of women's basketball to unprecedented levels in both the college ranks and the WNBA, and Tuesday she was named the AP Female Athlete of the Year for her impact on and off the court.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Embiid ejected after drawing 2 technicals in game against Wembanyama and Spurs
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Philadelphia 76ers star Joel Embiid was ejected in the first half of Monday night’s 111-106 victory over the San Antonio Spurs after drawing two technical fouls. Referee Jenna Schroeder ejected Embiid with 2:59 left in the second quarter.
item.dc:creator: AARON BRACY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Wemby at The Garden. LeBron vs. Steph. The NBA's Christmas Day lineup, as always, has star power
item.description: LeBron James made his Christmas debut in 2003. Victor Wembanyama was born 10 days later. That's right: James has been featured on the NBA's big day for longer than Wembanyama has been alive.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tales from the tour in a year filled with medals and tears and a few digs
item.description: Scottie Scheffler won a lot of tournaments and had to do a lot of talking this year. One theme stood out even as Scheffler was dominating his competition by winning nine times. The answer could be found on a white board in Paris.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bills' Sean McDermott avoids making airplane analogy for team after 9/11 reference controversy
item.description: Buffalo Bills head coach Sean McDermott did his best to avoid making an airplane reference while talking about his team's first-half performance against the New England Patriots.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Could SF Giants upgrade at first base? Following eventful weekend, their options are growing slim
item.description: Buster Posey said the Giants will keep their options open at first base, but how many options are available following an eventful weekend?
item.dc:creator: Justice delos Santos, The Mercury News

item.title: Salesian dominates Dougherty Valley in matchup of marquee East Bay programs
item.description: Bay Area boys basketball: Salesian dominates Dougherty Valley to pick up statement win early in season.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Canilao, The Mercury News

item.title: Cavs' Jarrett Allen stuns fans with backward halfcourt shot before game
item.description: Cleveland Cavaliers star Jarrett Allen wowed the crowd at the Rocket Mortgage Fieldhouse on Monday night ahead of the game against the Utah Jazz
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GJ Tigers Grant Lewis and Will Weirath shine under varsity spotlight
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—Grant Lewis and Will Weirath aren’t your average freshmen. They’re not just competing with older, more experienced players — they’re excelling. Their combined 25-point performance in the Tigers’ recent win highlights their remarkable season.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: John Seidler takes over as control person of Padres after Peter's death
item.description: John Seidler, an entrepreneur and business executive, has been named the next control person of the San Diego Padres, pending approval by Major League Baseball, after the death of his brother Peter Seidler, the team's former owner.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Lamar Jackson and the Ravens draw even atop the AFC North, beating the 1st-place Steelers 34-17
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Lamar Jackson was solid in Baltimore's biggest game of the season so far. Then, after one of the star quarterback's rare slip-ups, one of his teammates delivered the play of the day.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jazz win back-to-back games for first time this season
item.description: BROOKLYN, N.Y. (ABC4 Sports) - For the first time since April, the Utah Jazz are on a winning streak. Lauri Markkanen scored 21 points, Collin Sexton and Svi Mykhailiuk each had 18 points, and the Utah Jazz beat the Brooklyn Nets 105-94 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Mahomes throws for TD and runs for score as Chiefs beat Texans 27-19 to close in on No. 1 seed
item.description: Patrick Mahomes knew he could run on the ankle he sprained last week. The bigger concern for the Chiefs quarterback was how he would stop, when all the weight and pressure and momentum would land squarely upon it.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oakland legend, Hall of Famer Rickey Henderson has died at the age of 65
item.description: (KRON) – Rickey Henderson, one of the greatest baseball players to ever live, died at age 65. Henderson is Major League Baseball’s all-time stolen base leader and is considered by many to be the greatest player in A’s franchise history.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Bulls beat Celtics 117-108 in Boston
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Celtics forward Jaylen Brown took issue with back-to-back technical fouls called on coach Joe Mazzulla and himself late in the fourth quarter of Boston’s 117-108 loss to the Chicago Bulls on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Brock Purdy gets his offensive line new cars for Christmas
item.description: (KRON) -- It's the most wonderful time of the year -- especially if you're a San Francisco 49ers offensive lineman. Quarterback Brock Purdy hooked the big dawgs up with brand-new Toyota cars, a video posted by the 49ers on Thursday showed.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: NCAA president says there are 'less than 10' transgender athletes in college sports
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker told a Senate panel that there are fewer than 10 transgender athletes he is aware of who currently compete in college sports, pouring cold water on an issue Republicans have said is a nationwide problem and one that is increasingly fraught territory for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Donato caps Blackhawks comeback, Blackhawks top Capitals 3-2
item.description: Ryan Donato scored his team-leading 11th goal with 2:18 left in regulation to cap Chicago's three-goal, third-period rally and lift the Blackhawks to a 3-2 win over the Washington Capitals on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: This is when you'll be able to start watching San Diego FC live
item.description: San Diego FC on Tuesday released its full preseason schedule for the 2025 Major League Soccer season.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Florida Gators look to tie a bow on season in Gasparilla Bowl against Tulane
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — The Union Home Mortgage Gasparilla Bowl hosts the Tulane Green Wave (9-4) representing the American Athletic Conference and the University of Florida Gators (7-5) from the Southeastern Conference.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White Sox announce list of players to attend SoxFest 2025
item.description: The Chicago White Sox have announced a list of seven players and eight prospects who will join fans at SoxFest 2025, which is making its return after a four-year hiatus, featuring new fan experiences and a "State of the White Sox" address with Manager Will Venable and front office leadership.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9
